title: ConceptManifest
description: Output of surface-planning. Maps how a concept's actions and state surface to users per channel.

channel.description: The channel key through which the concept surfaces. Open string; when a channels.json registry is present, must be a registered key (e.g. web-ui, cli, mcp, api, http-rest, webhook).

actions.description: Affordances: how each canonical concept action is invoked on this channel. Each entry references one action and describes its channel-specific projection.

action.description: Action name (must match an action in the concept definition).

element.description: The affordance: what the user sees or types (button, command, tool descriptor, route, slash command).

label.description: The display text or command syntax.

exclusions.description: Actions intentionally not surfaced on this channel, each with a reason. Satisfies the coverage rule for an action that has no sensible affordance here.

action.description: Action name (must match an action in the concept definition).

reason.description: Why this action is not surfaced on this channel (e.g. 'interactive hover gesture; no CLI analogue').

component.description: State component name (must match a component in the concept definition).

element.description: How the state is made visible.

visibility.description: When the state is shown to the user.

op_hint.description: How the user discovers the operational principle through this channel.

gaps.description: Actions or state with no surface, or surfaces that are inadequate.
